assertions.description: window.performance exists — the host object the proposed getSpeculations() instance method hangs off.

performance.getSpeculations is a function — an INSTANCE method on the performance object (never a static on the Performance constructor), exposed when the SpeculationMeasurement runtime feature is on.
The surface is not a static on the Performance constructor — Performance.getSpeculations must not exist as a function (the binding is the instance object, per the experimental Chromium IDL).
The current explainer/IDL surface is a method, not the stale performance.speculations property name retained in some trial metadata; that property must be undefined.
When exposed, getSpeculations() returns an object whose preloads, preconnects and navigations members are arrays and which carries the navigationDestinationURL member (USVString?, so it may be null).
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof performance.getSpeculations !== 'function') return false; const s = performance.getSpeculations(); return !!s && typeof s === 'object' && Array.isArray(s.preloads) && Array.isArray(s.preconnects) && Array.isArray(s.navigations) && 'navigationDestinationURL' in s; })()

assertions.description: When exposed, navigationDestinationURL is either a string (a same-origin destination) or null — the nullable USVString's JS absence value is null, never undefined (exposure shape itself is provisional in the explainer).
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof performance.getSpeculations !== 'function') return false; const v = performance.getSpeculations().navigationDestinationURL; return v === null || typeof v === 'string'; })()

assertions.description: When exposed, every reported preload entry has a string url and a used value that is either null (not consumed at read time) or a number (DOMHighResTimeStamp of consumption). Null is only definitive waste when the page lifetime ends; pagehide may instead enter the BFCache.
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof performance.getSpeculations !== 'function') return false; return performance.getSpeculations().preloads.every(p => p && typeof p.url === 'string' && (p.used === null || typeof p.used === 'number')); })()
